reviews.review_description: Seems good, and good value but would be even better if snooker table was full size and could have a smooth rotation. Otherwise all other features are excellent, thanks.

This new version of Cueist unfortunately continues the tradition of the first Cueist that came out for the first Quest: The smooth navigation movement is nauseating. Also, aiming of shots is awkward and makes it hard to be precise.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for giving it a try!
In the latest update, you can switch movement types by simply clicking the left thumbstick. I've also added a laser-type pointer to help with aiming.

reviews.review_description: my friend and I picked this up and had fun in it. probably one of the better pool games I've tried for the quest. the controls and gameplay were easy to pick up and learn. even like the idea of trying it with a real pool cue in hand as it listed as an option. I don't know if it goes up to more than 2 players or not now, but if not, I hope that's planned down the road. give it more of a hangout bar feel with more ppl. my only complain is I wish the table had more friction to it. the balls just slide like glass sometimes., but that could just take some getting used to the controls. all in all a fun game for the price.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, and I'm glad you're having fun with it!
A few people have mentioned disliking the current physics (I was going for an "artistic billiards" feel) so I'll add an option to switch to different physics models in the next update.

reviews.review_description:
Could be great but the physics just feels wrong. See Black Hole Pool is see how it should be done.

Update:
Great to see the 8 physics options in the latest update. Excellent. 👍🏻

reviews.review_title: Has Snooker and I love it!🤩
reviews.review_description:
To this date. I think this is the only vr game which have snooker on it. I've been searching for ages. I bought it for playing snooker and it's good. Better if the table is full size. Or already give the option for the full size or smaller one. I hope there's a way to remove the ghost ball too. I just need the aim guideline. 

Playing against the Snooker AI too is not that good though. I hope the snooker AI Herts improved upon too. I play Vs myself and it's pretty fun though. There is multiplayer but have yet to see someone playing online to play with. I hope it continues to gets updated also.😉

reviews.review_title: Room to improve - please do so
reviews.review_description: I already played Cueist on the Go and found it very nice there.  The game mechanics were basically easier to handle than here on the quest.  What I really miss there and here is that there is no background atmosphere.  No pub feeling.  In addition, the shots here are difficult to dose and usually too strong.  Still better than Black Hole Pool in my eyes.  However, the atmosphere there is very nice.  I also like being able to watch YouTube there.  I hope for progress.

Played cueist  on the go and find this oculus version much more comfortable to play. Graphics are well done…the game is quite easy to get use to the mechanics…..believe the devs have put a lot of time into this…also think the back ground has plenty of atmosphere. …reminds when I use to go to snooker hall with my dad …well done devs for this.  Ps what a silly low price!!!

The game is fun, the pool table and balls look decent. The location could look and feel a nicer, maybe have a bar setting with some ambient noise (mumbling, glasses clinking, etc.). I am uncertain where the game sources it's radio stations, but I was only able to get foreign radio stations in languages I could not understand. Pointing the radio player to a YouTube video or playlist via a browser of some sorts would be a nice addition.

Unfortunately, the game is very buggy. Multiple times the game would announce that it is my (player 1) turn to break, but the AI (player 2) would go ahead and break, making the game announce a foul and hand the turn over to player 2, essentially having the AI play a foul in my name. 

The AI often aims directly for one of their balls, it doesn't care if there is a foreign or the 8 ball obstructing the path. If it doesn't hit a foul, it basically just continues to pocket their balls.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! Yeah that... should not be happening. It's not something I've seen before but I'll do some digging. Expect a fix for it within a week or so. The other requests will take a bit more time, but are on the roadmap.

reviews.review_description:
Me and my brother bought this game but can’t game together online. When is gonna be available?
In other things I like very much the game, the only no real thing is you can hit the ball too hard, more much than real life, think it’s no real

developer_response.body: Hi Rafa, thanks for the feedback! I think you also tried to get in touch through my website? Apologies for not getting back to you sooner. Anyway, you should definitely be able to play with your brother. As long as you are already friends, he should show up in your list of available players. After accepting an invite, it can take some time to connect properly, so just wait a little while and it should connect.

reviews.review_description:
Bought this a few weeks ago but got a refund.Bought again the other day and was blown away.Different physics settings and more make this my new goto pool game.I have 1 question tho,how to change the cpu level as seems stuck on level 20.Also a teleport option would be much appreciated,love it tho.
UPDATED REVIEW
still no change in cpu level but confident theres a reason.

developer_response.body: Thanks for giving it another try! The CPU level goes up whenever you beat the CPU in a match, so you'll eventually be playing against level 1 (the top)... which is still not a fantastic player but I'm working on improving that too.

reviews.review_description:
Tried a few pool games in the past and didn't really enjoy the experience. This was the best I have tried so far, but it isn't just quite there yet and needs work in some areas.
Other have said that the balls run too quick and indeed they do. However tournament tables do play quicker then tables the normal person plays on.  But still I did a scr** shot not too hard, enough to stun it a little and slight return and if flew around the table.

Rising the table to preferred height is easy, but for me too easy to accidentally change it while turning.

Had a few glitches with doing a gentle shot and it going like I had hit it full pelt.

The hand position seemed a lot better/more natural then other games I have tried.

Getting the right shot can be a pain as no aim to show angle, you press grip on left controller to see that. If you need to adjust shot, you release the grip button and move cue how you think it should be, press the grip button to see shot. Can be finicky, though I expect I might need more practice as well.

For the price it is worth having, but would be so much better if the physics was  balanced a little bit.

I have tried several pool games and found them very disappointing until Cueist 2. 

Being the newest I think it has done better than all the others. 

Once you have a multi player added it should be great.  

Suggestions:  Make the table a solid. Being able to walk into it takes away from the reality aspect.  I've played air hockey games that had a solid table. 

When playing solo, get rid of the two player game play.  Most don't want to play both player parts. Make  it like a practice mode.

Players need more options, like turning on/off the voice over.   

Would be great to have music playing while having a game.  Maybe add spotify or other methods to select tunes.

It would add atmosphere if the video games had sound. 

The left hand when holding the tip of the cue doesn't look correct. The cue should be between the two fingers.

For the price, this pool shooter offers a good variety of game play and options. The Cueist 2 table option is too fast and needs to be slowed down some. The Snooker table is not a full scale Snooker table. It would be nice if they included tutorials on how to play the various games. Multiplayer mode needs to be worked on for better connection with other players.

I'm really impressed with this so far. I'd really like to try using a real cue but I can't find any information on how to do it. Also, I wonder if hand tracking can ever be incorporated?

Please tell me how to use a real cue! Thanks
